title: ModelState.IsValid should be called in controller actions
description: This rule enforces the developer to validate the model within a controller action, ensuring the application’s robustness and reliability. Querying the ModelState.IsValid property is necessary because it checks if the submitted data in the HTTP request is valid or not. This property evaluates all the validation attributes applied on your model properties and determines whether the data provided satisfies those validation rules.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

never_trigger_on: GET methods with simple parameters (int, string)
Methods that already contain ModelState.IsValid validation
logic.reasoning: Uses custom function to detect controller action methods with HTTP attributes ([HttpPost], [HttpPut], [HttpPatch], [HttpDelete]) that do not validate ModelState.IsValid. The function analyzes the relationship between Attribute nodes and MethodDeclaration nodes to identify HTTP action methods, then checks if they contain ModelState validation logic. Excludes test methods, GET methods with simple parameters, and methods that already have validation.

should_trigger: [HttpPost] public IActionResult CreateUser(UserModel user) { SaveUser(user); return Ok(); }
[HttpPut] public IActionResult UpdateUser(UserModel user) { UpdateUser(user); return Ok(); }
[HttpPatch] public IActionResult PatchUser(UserModel user) { PatchUser(user); return Ok(); }
should_not_trigger: [HttpPost] public IActionResult CreateUser(UserModel user) { if (!ModelState.IsValid) return BadRequest(); SaveUser(user); return Ok(); }
[HttpGet] public IActionResult GetUser(int id) { return Ok(GetUser(id)); }
public IActionResult HelperMethod(UserModel user) { return Ok(); }
